page: Microsoft Azure D96as_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DASv4 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this server features 96 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 48 physical cores with multi-threading enabled. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is provided via a 786 GB local disk with Premium Storage capability, and there are no bundled GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core CPU testing placing in the top 10% of all servers. The system also registers top-tier results in PassMark memory scores and CPU encryption tests, alongside strong multi-core Geekbench scores and clang compilation speeds. Single-core performance is strong, though large-block memory bandwidth and single-threaded compression tasks fall into the average range. Large-model LLM inference speeds also remain average, while smaller model prompt processing and medium-model text generation achieve strong marks.

From a resource density perspective, the D96as_v4 offers qualitative cost efficiency for highly parallelized workloads that require a balanced ratio of compute to memory without the overhead of dedicated GPU hardware. The combination of 96 dedicated vCPUs and Premium Storage capability makes this instance well-suited for relational database operations, multi-threaded software compilation, static web serving, and medium-scale text processing or image processing pipelines.
description: Microsoft Azure D96as_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DASv4 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture under Microsoft Hyper-V, it features 96 dedicated vCPUs across 48 physical cores, 384.0 GB of RAM, and a 786 GB local storage drive with Premium Storage support. The server delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance, high-speed encryption, and strong memory scores in benchmark testing. Single-core processing and large-block memory bandwidth perform at average levels. Lacking a GPU, it relies entirely on its AMD EPYC processor for computation. This hardware profile is optimized for highly parallelized workloads, including database operations, multi-threaded software compilation, static web serving, and image processing pipelines.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D96as_v4 is a general-purpose VM featuring 96 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and 786 GB local storage, delivering top-tier multi-core and encryption performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D96as_v4 is a 96-vCPU general-purpose VM with 384 GB RAM and 786 GB local storage, powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor.
tagline: A high-density, 96-vCPU general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processing and premium local storage capabilities.

384.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core ratio
786 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier multi-core CPU performance and high-speed cryptographic encryption
